content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Jasper E. Hempel 
Hempel & Hempel 
1121 L Street, Suite 402 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Dear Mr. Hempel: 
May 13, 1986 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-86-156 
This is to confirm the advice provided to you last month by 
Colleen McGee of this office regarding the termination of your 
status as a lobbyist. 
As I understand the facts, you ceased all activities as a 
lobbyist and ceased receiving payments for lobbying as of 
December 31, 1985. 
However, because of some confusion resulting 
from the special re-registration process conducted earlier this 
year, you did not file a Statement of Termination. 
You asked if 
you must complete the 1986 registration forms and file a 
disclosure report for the first quarter of 1986 in order to 
terminate at this time. 
Ms. McGee advised you, and this will confirm, that you may 
file the enclosed Statement of Termination (Form 606) at this 
time and may use December 31, 1985, as the date of termination. 
No registration statements or first quarter disclosure repo·rts 
need be filed for 1986 so long as you did not engage in lobbying 
activities and did not receive any payments for lobbying after 
December 31, 1985. 
I hope this information has been helpful. 
Please call me 
at (916) 322-5662 if you have additional questions. 
CJW:cah 
Sincerely, 
C tbU$L '-~f../'_' k'\..cL L ~Lt-U 
Carla J. Wardlow 
Political Reform Consultant 
cc: 
Office of the Secretary of 
State, Political Reform Division 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

sections.question: Must you complete the 1986 registration forms and file a disclosure report for the first quarter of 1986 in order to terminate your status as a lobbyist at this time?
sections.conclusion: You may file the enclosed Statement of Termination (Form 606) at this time and may use December 31, 1985, as the date of termination. No registration statements or first quarter disclosure reports need be filed for 1986 so long as you did not engage in lobbying activities and did not receive any payments for lobbying after December 31, 1985.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a lobbyist who ceased all lobbying activities and payments as of December 31, 1985, but did not file a Statement of Termination due to confusion from a special re-registration process, must file 1986 registration statements and first quarter disclosure reports.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advised that the lobbyist may file a Statement of Termination retroactively dated December 31, 1985, and is not required to file 1986 registration statements or first quarter disclosure reports, provided no lobbying activities or payments occurred after that date.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Letter confirms previously provided oral advice; no formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present, but question and conclusion are clearly identifiable in letter body; document is a confirmation of prior advice given by staff member Colleen McGee.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Must you complete the 1986 registration forms and file a disclosure report for the first quarter of 1986 in order to terminate your status as a lobbyist at this time?

CONCLUSION: You may file the enclosed Statement of Termination (Form 606) at this time and may use December 31, 1985, as the date of termination. No registration statements or first quarter disclosure reports need be filed for 1986 so long as you did not engage in lobbying activities and did not receive any payments for lobbying after December 31, 1985.

embedding.summary: FPPC confirms advice to Jasper Hempel regarding termination of his lobbyist status, permitting him to file a retroactive Statement of Termination dated December 31, 1985, without filing 1986 registration or disclosure reports.
